title: Single Agent Tradeoffs
concept: Lower cost, lower latency, and simpler state; but harder to prompt-engineer and prune context.

exercises.prompt: What is a single agent design?

options.text: A design using one complex agent
A design using multiple specialized agents
A design that avoids any agents completely

exercises.explain: A single agent design focuses on using one complex AI to handle tasks, while a multi-agent design relies on several specialized AIs.

cards.title: Single Agent Tradeoffs
cards.body: A single agent means you have one AI doing all the work, which can simplify some things but also brings challenges.

cards.title: What is Single Agent Design?
cards.body: Single agent design involves using one AI model to perform various tasks. This can lead to easier management and lower costs.

cards.title: Single Agent as a One-Person Band
cards.body: Imagine a one-person band playing different instruments. They can create diverse music but may struggle to play complex pieces compared to a full band.

cards.title: Example of Lower Cost
cards.body: Using one AI for several tasks can save money on development and maintenance, unlike having multiple AIs.

cards.title: Myth: All Tasks Are Easier with One AI
cards.body: Many believe one AI is always better, but handling complex tasks might be harder without teamwork.

cards.title: Comparing Single vs Multi-Agent
cards.body: Single agents can be faster and cheaper, but also harder to manage.

left.text: Lower cost, lower latency, simpler state

right.text: More complex, higher cost, teamwork needed

exercises.prompt: What is a benefit of using a single agent design?
exercises.explain: Lower latency means a single agent can respond more quickly since it's handling everything on its own.

exercises.prompt: Single agents always perform better than multi-agent designs.
exercises.explain: While single agents have benefits, multi-agent designs can outperform them in specific situations.

exercises.prompt: A single agent design has a ______ cost compared to multiple agents.
exercises.sentence: A single agent design has a lower cost compared to multiple agents.
exercises.explain: Lower cost is a key advantage of single agent designs compared to using multiple agents.

exercises.prompt: Which statement reflects a tradeoff of a single agent design?

options.text: Single agents can be simpler to develop but harder to adapt.
Single agents always outperform multi-agent teams.
Single agents are always more expensive than team designs.

exercises.explain: This statement captures the essence of tradeoffs between simplicity and adaptability in single agent designs.

exercises.prompt: What is a common challenge when using a single agent design?

options.text: Difficulty in prompting the AI correctly

exercises.explain: Difficulty in prompting the AI correctly is a tradeoff that arises because one complex agent needs precise instructions to perform well.
